headlines.title: Iran war live: Trump, Tehran at odds over nuclear inspections, Hormuz
headlines.snippet: The US and Iran have issued conflicting statements regarding nuclear inspections and the Strait of Hormuz. Negotiators are reportedly working towards a comprehensive deal.

headlines.title: UN begins evacuation of 11,000 sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.snippet: The UN's maritime agency has initiated an operation to evacuate over 11,000 sailors who are stranded in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.title: Protesters sentenced to decades in US prison over alleged antifa ties
headlines.snippet: Civil liberties advocates are concerned about the sentencing of protesters, who received decades in a US prison, with alleged ties to antifa. The case involved a protest outside Prairieland Detention Center.

headlines.title: US Supreme Court says Rastafarian man shaved by prison guards can’t sue
headlines.snippet: The high court has ruled that prisoners cannot sue prison staff for monetary damages under religious liberties law. This decision affects a Rastafarian man who was shaved by prison guards.

headlines.title: Israel’s deliberate targeting of Palestinian children
headlines.snippet: A new UN report details the impact of Israel's war on Gaza and its occupation of the West Bank on Palestinian children. The report documents the toll on these children.

headlines.title: Israel denying Palestinian women medical care in prison
headlines.snippet: Palestinian women in Israel's Damon Prison are facing dire conditions, including overcrowding and deliberate medical neglect, according to the Palestinian Prisoners and Ex-Prisoners Affairs Commission. Pregnant prisoners are reportedly denied necessary medical care and basic necessities.

headlines.title: Ben-Gvir says Israel may ‘act alone’ against Iran in split with US
headlines.snippet: Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir suggested Israel might need to act independently against Iran's nuclear program, stating the US would be naive to expect Iran to abandon it. He emphasized Israel's responsibility to confront the Iranian threat.

headlines.title: UN report: Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children
headlines.snippet: A UN commission of inquiry has found that Israel committed genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting Palestinian children. The report indicates that children continue to be killed and injured despite a ceasefire.

headlines.title: Oman announces temporary maritime corridor to bypass Hormuz blockade
headlines.snippet: Oman has announced a temporary maritime corridor to facilitate vessel passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Vessels will need to coordinate with the International Maritime Organisation and Omani authorities.

headlines.title: UN report: 80 percent of Gaza living in dire conditions
headlines.snippet: A UN assessment shows that approximately 80 percent of Gaza's population, nearly 1.7 million displaced Palestinians, face severe shortages of water, shelter, and basic services. Many are housed in overcrowded shelters or sleep outdoors.

headlines.title: United States Of America
headlines.snippet: This item refers to the United States of America, as reported by Al Arabiya English.

headlines.title: Without its missiles, Iran would be ‘just like Gaza,’ president says
headlines.snippet: The president stated that without its missiles, Iran would be comparable to Gaza.

headlines.title: Rubio Says Conflicts Won’t End if Iran’s Proxies Keep Firing
headlines.snippet: Rubio commented that conflicts will persist if Iran's proxies continue their actions.

headlines.title: Rubio Says US Will Not Accept Iranian Tolls on Hormuz
headlines.snippet: Rubio indicated that the US will not accept any tolls imposed by Iran on the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.title: Meloni Calls For a Return to Normality After Trump Spat
headlines.snippet: Meloni called for a return to normalcy following a dispute with Trump.

headlines.title: Rubio rejects Iran tolls on Hormuz as deal strains multiply
headlines.snippet: Rubio asserted that no nation can impose tolls on the Strait of Hormuz, while Iran's lead negotiator stated the passage will not revert to its pre-war status.

headlines.title: US Congress passes symbolic Iran war rebuke to Trump
headlines.snippet: The US Congress passed a resolution that symbolically rebukes Trump, directing him to remove US forces from hostilities with Iran unless explicitly authorized by Congress.

headlines.title: Families say Lebanese detainees 'forgotten' in Israel talks
headlines.snippet: Families are concerned that Lebanese detainees are being overlooked amidst ongoing talks, with at least 37 people detained by Israeli forces. Negotiations resumed in Washington.

headlines.title: Sudan city 'weeks' from catastrophe: UN official
headlines.snippet: A UN official warned that a city in Sudan is weeks away from catastrophe and faces an imminent risk of mass atrocities. The UN urged the RSF to withdraw.

headlines.title: Pro-Israel lobby boasts of role in US sanctions on UN's Albanese
headlines.snippet: Hillel Neuer stated that he led a campaign resulting in US sanctions against Francesca Albanese, a UN Special Rapporteur, due to her pro-Palestinian advocacy.

headlines.title: Should Egypt remove ‘marital status’ from women’s ID cards?
headlines.snippet: The article discusses how certain options in Egypt's national ID cards' 'marital status' field can legitimize harassment, restrict housing, or impede a woman's career, treating personal data as a weaponized stigma.

headlines.title: The future of oil prices may depend on China
headlines.snippet: The future of oil prices may hinge on China, the world's second-largest crude oil consumer, as the US and Iran negotiate the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. China has actively sought to secure supplies.

headlines.title: Why the forces that felled Keir Starmer threaten so many Western leaders
headlines.snippet: The article suggests that British Prime Minister Keir Starmer's downfall reflects a broader challenge faced by Western leaders: the failure to deliver tangible change and regain voter trust in politics' ability to improve lives.

headlines.title: ‘What ceasefire?’: In northern Israel, locals doubt an agreement can end the war with Hezbollah
headlines.snippet: Locals in Metula, northern Israel, express doubt that any agreement can end the war with Hezbollah, with businesses and the town remaining largely deserted.

headlines.title: Israel must have “arms independence,” Netanyahu says, as US military aid expiring in 2028
headlines.snippet: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stated that Israel must achieve "arms independence" as significant US military aid is set to expire in 2028, emphasizing the need for self-reliance.
